gene_region.label: G6PD transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical G6PD genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window relative to the reverse-strand transcription start, used to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: G6PD encodes glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase, the rate-limiting pentose phosphate pathway enzyme. This bundle emphasizes NADPH production, red-cell oxidative stress defense, glutathione recycling, and hemolysis susceptibility.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local G6PD bundle is red-cell oxidative metabolism oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls.
variant_effect_overview: G6PD variants can alter or tag NADPH production, red-cell oxidative stress defense, glutathione recycling, and hemolysis susceptibility; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
Common markers, pharmacogenetic alleles, structural events, and high-impact pathogenic variants should be kept separate during interpretation.
condition_research_overview: Pentose phosphate pathway and NADPH biology
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: G6PD methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a NADPH production, red-cell oxidative stress defense, glutathione recycling, and hemolysis susceptibility gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for G6PD.
Because G6PD biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.
Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against NADPH production, red-cell oxidative stress defense, glutathione recycling, and hemolysis susceptibility.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use G6PD promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for NADPH production, red-cell oxidative stress defense, glutathione recycling, and hemolysis susceptibility.

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000160211: G6PD coordinate source

biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a G6PD red-cell oxidative metabolism thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to NADPH production, red-cell oxidative stress defense, glutathione recycling, and hemolysis susceptibility and G6PD deficiency review context, Drug/food-triggered hemolysis susceptibility. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs1050828 (G6PD A- marker) is curated as a G6PD marker for G6PD deficiency review context, Drug/food-triggered hemolysis susceptibility. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the NADPH production, red-cell oxidative stress defense, glutathione recycling, and hemolysis susceptibility pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag NADPH production, red-cell oxidative stress defense, glutathione recycling, and hemolysis susceptibility.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
associated_conditions: Pentose phosphate pathway and NADPH biology
research_context: Use this marker as a G6PD pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair rs1050828 (G6PD A- marker) with G6PD methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.
